title: Atoms, Molecules and Electronic Configuration
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Understand Bohr's model of the atom with K, L, M, N electron shells ($2n^2$).
Calculate valence electrons and atomic numbers for Sodium (Z=11), Carbon (Z=6), Oxygen (Z=8).
Inspect elements interactively on the Periodic Table.

objectives.text: Select Sodium (Z=11) and view electronic distribution [2, 8, 1].
Identify the valence electron in the outermost M shell.

theory: Bohr's atomic model proposes that electrons revolve around the nucleus in discrete energy orbits (K, L, M, N). Maximum shell capacity is $2n^2$.
hints: Use the Atomic Number slider or click on the Periodic Table grid to select elements.
Observe the glowing blue valence electron ring in the outer shell.

tasks.instruction: Select an element with Atomic Number Z > 1.

observationPrompts: Why do noble gases like Helium (Z=2) and Neon (Z=10) have complete outer shells?

realWorldExamples.title: Chemical Reactivity
realWorldExamples.description: Valence electrons dictate how elements form ionic and covalent bonds with other elements.

commonMistakes: Exceeding the maximum $2n^2$ electron capacity of inner K and L shells.
